question_text: Antiwar movements test citizenship by asking
answer: when dissent against state violence is a responsibility rather than disloyalty

choices.text: that antiwar and civil-rights movements never illustrate civic ideology as a general historical claim
that collaborative civic projects cannot cross knowledge traditions in this reading of the source
when dissent against state violence is a responsibility rather than disloyalty
that it follows responsible citizenship ignores global humanitarian concerns

explanation: Antiwar movements are a listed conflict context.
common_mistake: Students brand all antiwar dissent as treason automatically.

skill_tested: Reading an unemployment figure from a comparative table
question_text: An economic-system chart in the stem shows unemployment rates: Marketland 6%; Mixland 7%; Commandland 4% (official); Tradland not reported. What unemployment percentage is shown for Mixland?
common_mistake: Students average the numbers or pick Marketland.

question_text: A movement argues that modern welfare liberalism should be rolled back toward stronger markets and traditional social norms. The challenge to modern liberalism most accurately labelled is

choices.text: that Stalin-era planning abolished unchecked political authority as a general historical claim
that class analysis has no place in resistance-to-liberalism debates as a general historical claim
that it follows command economies maximize private property and free enterprise

explanation: Outcome 2.12k lists Aboriginal collective thought, environmentalism, religious perspectives, neo-conservatism, postmodernism, and extremism
common_mistake: Students confuse neo-conservatism with classical liberalism or with Soviet communism automatically.

question_text: A fishery council sets catch limits so the stock survives for the whole community. This practice most directly reflects which principle of collectivism?

choices.text: that one must conclude ideology is only empty slogans with no effect on choices
that it follows personal identity and political ideology never interact
that examining spectrum positions is useless for source analysis across every liberal democracy

explanation: Outcome 1.8k lists collective responsibility, collective interest, cooperation, economic equality, adherence to collective norms, and public property.
common_mistake: Students confuse collective interest (shared benefit) with economic equality (narrowed material gaps).

question_text: Factory owners resist safety rules as interference with market self-regulation. This development is best labelled as connected to

choices.text: that the Invisible Hand metaphor endorses central price controls across every liberal democracy
that industrial capitalism played no role in liberal critiques of tradition
that one must conclude free markets require the abolition of all contracts and property

explanation: Outcome 2.6k examines laissez-faire capitalism, industrialization, class system, and limited government as impacts of classical liberal thought
common_mistake: Students misapply Cold War or contemporary Canadian labels to nineteenth-century laissez-faire capitalism.
